results.title: Current in a circuit
results.value: What is the SI unit of electric current?

hints.value: Think about the scientist after whom this unit is named.

results.title: Series circuits
results.value: In a series circuit with two identical bulbs, what happens to the brightness of each bulb compared to a circuit with only one bulb?

choices.value: One bulb is bright, one is off

hints.value: Consider how adding more resistance affects the current.

results.title: Weight vs Mass
results.value: What is the difference between mass and weight?

choices.value: Mass is the amount of matter; weight is the gravitational force on that mass
Weight is measured in kilograms; mass is measured in Newtons
Mass changes on the Moon; weight does not

hints.value: Weight is a force, measured in Newtons. Mass is measured in kilograms.

results.title: Energy conservation
results.value: State the law of conservation of energy.

hints.value: Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transferred between stores.

results.title: Wave properties
results.value: What is the frequency of a wave?

choices.value: The number of complete waves passing a point per second
